steps.title: Recognize that adding equations eliminates y
steps.content: The first equation is $x + 10 = -9y + 5$ and the second is $x - 10 = 9y + 7$. The coefficients of $y$ are $-9$ and $+9$ — they are opposites. Adding the two equations will eliminate $y$.

steps.title: Add the equations to find 2x
steps.content:
Add left sides: $(x + 10) + (x - 10) = 2x$
Add right sides: $(-9y + 5) + (9y + 7) = 12$
Therefore $2x = 12$. That matches choice C — C is correct.
